items.title: Persistent login survives reload
steps: 1. In the session, toggle the Browser pane on.
2. Navigate to a site with a login and sign in.
3. Reload the DevEasy browser tab (full page refresh).
4. Re-open the Browser pane for the same session.
items.expected: The site is still logged in — the persistent Chromium profile survived the reload and the WS reattached.

items.title: Agent drives the same browser via MCP tools
items.precondition: Browser pane open for the active session.
steps: 1. Ask the agent in chat to navigate to a URL and click/read an element.
2. Watch the screencast while the agent acts.
3. Confirm the agent's tool calls appear in the transcript.
items.expected: The agent's browser_navigate/click/type/read_page actions drive the same Chromium shown in the pane; results are visible live and tool calls show in chat.
items.notes: Requires a live claude session performing the MCP handshake against /mcp/browser.

items.title: Close frees memory but keeps login
items.precondition: A logged-in browser open for the session.
steps: 1. Note the Chromium process RSS (e.g. via Activity Monitor / Task Manager).
2. Click the pane's close (×) button.
3. Confirm the Chromium process exits (RAM reclaimed).
4. Re-open the browser for the same session.
items.expected: Close terminates the Chromium process (memory freed); re-opening re-launches against the kept profile and the login is still present.

items.title: Deleting the session closes + wipes the browser
items.precondition: A browser open (or suspended) for a session with a profile on disk.
steps: 1. Note the profile dir at .deveasy-state/browser-profiles/<sessionId>.
2. Delete the session from the sidebar.
3. Inspect the process list and the profile dir.
items.expected: The session's Chromium is closed and .deveasy-state/browser-profiles/<sessionId> is removed (SessionsController.remove -> closeAndWipe).

items.title: Idle browser self-suspends
items.precondition: A browser open with no viewer attached and no agent activity.
steps: 1. Open a browser, then close the pane (detach the viewer) — or leave it untouched.
2. Wait past BROWSER_IDLE_MS (15 min) with no activity.
3. Check the process list.
items.expected: The idle Chromium is suspended (process killed, ~250MB reclaimed); the profile is kept so re-opening restores state.
items.notes: Timing check — lower BROWSER_IDLE_MS temporarily to verify faster.

items.title: Tabs + input-contention lock
steps: 1. Open a new tab, switch between tabs, close a tab.
2. While the agent is mid-action, click in the screencast.
3. Observe the controller indicator and the agent's next action.
items.expected: Tabs open/switch/close correctly; the indicator shows who is driving; an agent tool call during the human grace window returns BROWSER_HUMAN_DRIVING (agent yields to the operator).

items.title: Windows: clean teardown + persistence
items.precondition: DevEasy running on Windows with an open browser.
steps: 1. Open a browser and log into a site.
2. Quit DevEasy (Ctrl+C / close).
3. Check Task Manager for stray chrome.exe.
4. Restart DevEasy and re-open the browser.
items.expected: No orphaned chrome.exe after quit; login persists across restart. (macOS cannot verify this — Windows hardware run required.)
items.notes: Cannot be run from macOS; deferred to a Windows run (same pattern as the windows-native-support plan).

items.title: MCP endpoint is guarded and not swallowed by the SPA
steps: 1. POST /mcp/browser with no/invalid x-deveasy-token -> expect 403.
2. POST /mcp/browser from a non-loopback origin -> expect 403.
3. Confirm /mcp/browser is NOT served the SPA index.html (production build).
items.expected: Unauthorized MCP calls are refused (403); /mcp/* is excluded from the SPA fallback. (Auth-gate logic is also covered by Layer 1 unit tests in src/mcp/__tests__.)
items.notes: authorizeMcpRequest + token store are unit-tested; this item is the end-to-end HTTP assertion.

items.title: No regression in terminal or session streaming
steps: 1. Open a terminal in a project and run a command.
2. Start a session and send a turn; confirm streaming.
3. Confirm /ws/terminal and /ws/sessions still work alongside /ws/browser.
items.expected: Terminal and session streaming behave exactly as before; the new /ws/browser relay coexists.
